title: Iringol Kavu Temple
tagline: Iringol Kavu Temple — spiritual town of Kerala

overview.short: Iringole Kavu is a forest temple dedicated to Goddess Durga, situated in Kunnathunad Taluk of Ernakulam district, 2.5 km from Perumbavoor.
overview.description: Iringole Kavu is a forest temple dedicated to Goddess Durga, situated in Kunnathunad Taluk of Ernakulam district, 2.5 km from Perumbavoor. This is one of the 108 Durga Temples in Kerala believed to have been consecrated by Lord Parasurama, the sixth avatar of Lord Vishnu.

routes.byAir: Fly to nearest airport + road transfer

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: The Evangelistic Association of the East
topPlaces.description: The Evangelistic Association of the East is a registered non-governmental organization headquartered at Perumbavoor, Kerala working in the educational, social service and spirituality sectors. The organization consists of educational institutions, orphanages, and an archdiocese affiliated with Syriac Orthodox Church across South India.

image.alt: The Evangelistic Association of the East

topPlaces.name: Kuruppampady
topPlaces.description: Kuruppampady is situated about five kilometers from Perumbavoor on the Aluva-Munnar road, in Kunnathunad Taluk, Ernakulam district, Kerala, India.

topPlaces.name: St. Mary's Jacobite Syrian Cathedral, Kuruppampady
topPlaces.description: St. Mary's Cathedral, popularly known as Kuruppampady Pally is situated at Kuruppampady, 4 km east of Perumbavoor in the Kunnathunadu Taluk of Ernakulam district.

image.alt: St. Mary's Jacobite Syrian Cathedral, Kuruppampady

topPlaces.name: Rayamangalam, Kerala
topPlaces.description: Rayamangalam is a village (Grama punchyathu) in the Ernakulam district of Kerala state, India.

topPlaces.name: Pulluvazhy
topPlaces.description: Pulluvazhi is a small village in Rayamangalam Grama panchayat in the Ernakulam district of Kerala state, India. It is close to the towns Perumbavoor, Muvattupuzha and Kothamangalam.

topPlaces.name: Perumbavoor
topPlaces.description: Perumbavoor ([peɾumbaːʋuːr]) is a major town and municipality in the eastern part of the Kochi metropolitan area in the Indian state of Kerala. As per the 2011 Indian census, Perumbavoor has a population of 28,110 people and is situated on the banks of the Periyar River basin.

topPlaces.name: Manjummel
topPlaces.description: Manjummel is a satellite town of Eloor situated on Periyar river near Kochi, Kerala, India.

topPlaces.name: Thuruthiply
topPlaces.description: Thuruthiply is a town in Kunnathunad Taluk of Ernakulam district in the Indian state of Kerala. It is near Allapra on Perumbavoor-Kolenchery road.

The organization consists of educational institutions, orphanages, and an archdiocese affiliated with Syriac Orthodox Church across South India.
Kuruppampady is situated about five kilometers from Perumbavoor on the Aluva-Munnar road, in Kunnathunad Taluk, Ernakulam district, Kerala, India.

items.activity: St. Mary's Jacobite Syrian Cathedral, Kuruppampady
items.note: St. Mary's Cathedral, popularly known as Kuruppampady Pally is situated at Kuruppampady, 4 km east of Perumbavoor in the Kunnathunadu Taluk of Ernakulam district.
Rayamangalam is a village (Grama punchyathu) in the Ernakulam district of Kerala state, India.
Pulluvazhi is a small village in Rayamangalam Grama panchayat in the Ernakulam district of Kerala state, India. It is close to the towns Perumbavoor, Muvattupuzha and Kothamangalam.

Perumbavoor ([peɾumbaːʋuːr]) is a major town and municipality in the eastern part of the Kochi metropolitan area in the Indian state of Kerala. As per the 2011 Indian census, Perumbavoor has a population of 28,110 people and is situated on the banks of the Periyar River basin.
Manjummel is a satellite town of Eloor situated on Periyar river near Kochi, Kerala, India.
Thuruthiply is a town in Kunnathunad Taluk of Ernakulam district in the Indian state of Kerala. It is near Allapra on Perumbavoor-Kolenchery road.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Iringol Kavu Temple?
faq.a: The best time to visit Iringol Kavu Temple is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Iringol Kavu Temple?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Cochin International Airport, Kochi (12 km) and the nearest railway station is Ernakulam Junction (Kochi) (28 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Iringol Kavu Temple?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Iringol Kavu Temple at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Iringol Kavu Temple?
faq.a: Stays in Iringol Kavu Temple start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Iringol Kavu Temple?
faq.a: The top attractions are The Evangelistic Association of the East, Kuruppampady, St. Mary's Jacobite Syrian Cathedral, Kuruppampady, Rayamangalam, Kerala, Pulluvazhy.
